AdminUser.title: AdminUser
AdminUser.description: Represents an administrative user with access to the Banjarsari Connect dashboard for managing content and feedback. This entity does NOT store authentication credentials like passwords.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdminUser entity.

username.description: A unique username for the administrator, used for login identification.

email.description: The email address of the administrator for communication and identification.

role.description: The role or permission level of the administrator (e.g., 'SuperAdmin', 'ContentManager', 'FeedbackHandler').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the administrator's account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the administrator's account was last updated.

RWManagementMember.title: RWManagementMember
RWManagementMember.description: Represents a member of the RW 2 Banjarsari management team, with their profile and contact information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RWManagementMember entity.

name.description: The full name of the RW management team member.

role.description: The specific role or position held by the member within the RW management (e.g., 'Ketua RW', 'Sekretaris', 'Bendahara').

contactNumber.description: The primary contact phone number for the RW management member.

email.description: The email address for the RW management member.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the profile picture of the RW management member.

description.description: A short biographical description or summary of responsibilities for the RW management member.

adminUserId.description: Reference to the AdminUser entity if this management member also has a dashboard administration account. (Relationship: AdminUser 1:N RWManagementMember)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the RW management member's profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the RW management member's profile was last updated.

Announcement.title: Announcement
Announcement.description: Represents a news article or an important announcement published by the RW management for residents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Announcement entity.

title.description: The title of the news article or announcement.

content.description: The full body content of the news article or announcement.

summary.description: A concise summary of the announcement generated by the AI News Summarization Tool.

publicationDate.description: The date and time when the announcement was officially published.

status.description: The current status of the announcement (e.g., 'Draft', 'Published', 'Archived').

category.description: The category of the announcement (e.g., 'News', 'Event', 'Urgent Notice', 'Community Update').

imageUrl.description: URL to a featured image associated with the announcement, if any.

authorAdminUserId.description: Reference to the AdminUser who published or last updated this announcement. (Relationship: AdminUser 1:N Announcement)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the announcement was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the announcement was last updated.

ResidentFeedback.title: ResidentFeedback
ResidentFeedback.description: Captures aspirations, general feedback, or specific issue reports submitted by residents to the RW administration.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ResidentFeedback entity.

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the feedback or report was submitted by the resident.

type.description: The type of submission (e.g., 'Aspiration', 'General Feedback', 'Issue Report').

subject.description: A brief subject line summarizing the feedback or report.

message.description: The full detailed message or description provided by the resident.

residentName.description: The name of the resident submitting the feedback (optional, for anonymous submissions).

residentContactEmail.description: The email address of the resident for potential follow-up (optional).

residentContactPhone.description: The phone number of the resident for potential follow-up (optional).

status.description: The current processing status of the feedback (e.g., 'New', 'Received', 'In Progress', 'Resolved', 'Closed').

assignedToRWManagementMemberId.description: Reference to the RWManagementMember assigned to handle this feedback or report. (Relationship: RWManagementMember 1:N ResidentFeedback)

aiAnalysisSummary.description: A summary of the feedback generated by the AI Feedback Analysis Tool.

aiSentiment.description: The sentiment of the feedback identified by the AI (e.g., 'Positive', 'Neutral', 'Negative', 'Mixed').

aiIdentifiedThemes.description: An array of common themes or keywords identified in the feedback by the AI analysis tool.

adminNotes.description: Internal notes added by RW administration regarding the feedback or its resolution.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the resident feedback was received.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the resident feedback record was last updated (e.g., status change, admin notes added).

ImportantContact.title: ImportantContact
ImportantContact.description: Provides a searchable list of essential local contacts for residents, such as emergency services or local government.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ImportantContact entity.

name.description: The name of the organization, service, or individual (e.g., 'Puskesmas Banjarsari', 'Polsek Metro Utara').

category.description: The category of the contact (e.g., 'Emergency', 'Government Office', 'Health Services', 'Community Leader').

address.description: The physical address associated with the contact, if applicable.

websiteUrl.description: The official website URL for the contact, if available.

email.description: The email address for the contact, if available.

description.description: A brief description or additional information about the contact.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the important contact record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the important contact record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores administrative roles, where the document ID is the AdminUser's UID. This collection is used for DBAC (Database Access Control) to verify if a user is an administrator and their specific role. Includes denormalized 'role' for authorization independence.

params.name: adminUserId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the administrator.

definition.description: Publicly accessible profiles of RW management team members. Read access is open to all users, while write access is restricted to authenticated administrators. No denormalized authorization fields are required for read access.

params.name: memberId
params.description: The unique identifier for an RW Management Member.

definition.description: Master collection for administrators to manage all announcements, regardless of status (Draft, Published, Archived). Full read/write/delete access is restricted to administrators. The 'authorAdminUserId' field is for data integrity, not primary authorization. No denormalized authorization fields are required as access is based on the requesting user's global admin role.

params.name: announcementId
params.description: The unique identifier for an Announcement.

definition.description: Publicly accessible collection containing only announcements with 'Published' status. Read access is open to all users. Write access (for publishing/updating) is restricted to administrators. Documents in this collection are denormalized (copied) from '/announcements_management' when an announcement is published, ensuring QAPs for public list operations.

params.name: announcementId
params.description: The unique identifier for a published Announcement.

definition.description: Collection for storing resident feedback and reports. Anonymous users or residents can create new feedback. Read/update/delete access is strictly restricted to administrators. The 'assignedToRWManagementMemberId' field is for workflow, not primary authorization. No denormalized authorization fields are required as access is based on the requesting user's global admin role.

params.name: feedbackId
params.description: The unique identifier for a Resident Feedback submission.

definition.description: Publicly accessible list of important local contacts. Read access is open to all users. Write access is restricted to administrators. No denormalized authorization fields are required for read access.

params.name: contactId
params.description: The unique identifier for an Important Contact.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Banjarsari Connect prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The primary strategy involves **Structural Segregation** and **Authorization Independence via Denormalization**.

1.  **Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** Authorization is never dependent on fetching parent documents. For administrators, roles are explicitly stored in a dedicated top-level collection (`/admin_roles/{adminUserId}`). This allows security rules to use the efficient `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/admin_roles/$(request.auth.uid))` check, completely avoiding `get()` calls and enabling atomic operations.

2.  **Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):** Data with different security requirements are housed in separate collections. This simplifies security rules immensely, as all documents within a given collection share the same access patterns:
    *   **Public Data:** Collections like `/rw_management_members`, `/announcements_public`, and `/important_contacts` are designed for public read access. This makes `list` operations for residents (`QAPs`) inherently secure and performant.
    *   **Admin-Only Data:** Collections like `/admin_roles`, `/announcements_management`, and `/resident_feedback` are strictly for administrative access (read/write), ensuring sensitive data is protected.

3.  **Denormalization for QAPs and Atomic Operations:**
    *   **Admin Roles:** The existence of a document in `/admin_roles/{adminUserId}` denormalizes an administrator's status, allowing direct authorization checks without complex lookups.
    *   **Announcements:** To satisfy the requirement for both public news feeds and private administrative management, `Announcement` data is logically segregated. The `/announcements_management/{announcementId}` collection serves as the single source of truth for administrators to create, update, and manage all announcements (drafts, published, archived). When an announcement's status changes to 'Published', it is **denormalized (copied)** to the `/announcements_public/{announcementId}` collection. This ensures that the `/announcements_public` collection contains *only* published announcements, allowing any resident to securely `list` and `read` them without complex rule logic or performance overhead, directly addressing the QAPs principle. Administrators manage the master record in `/announcements_management` and trigger the replication to `/announcements_public` upon publish, enabling atomic updates to the public view.

4.  **Access Modeling:**
    *   **Global Roles (DBAC):** Implemented via `/admin_roles/{adminUserId}` for administrator authorization.
    *   **Public Data:** Collections like `/rw_management_members` and `/important_contacts` are public read.
    *   **Private Data:** `ResidentFeedback` and `Announcements_Management` are private to administrators.

This design minimizes rule complexity, maximizes security, and ensures that `list` operations (QAPs) are efficient and naturally adhere to security policies through structural clarity, rather than relying on complex in-rule filtering or cross-document checks.
